mission.title: Install and Configure K3s (Lightweight Kubernetes)
mission.description: K3s is a lightweight Kubernetes distribution designed for resource-constrained environments such as edge computing and IoT. It simplifies the installation process by packaging Kubernetes into a single binary, making it easy to deploy and manage. Key trade-offs include reduced resource usage and a smaller footprint, but it may lack some features found in full Kubernetes distributions.

steps.title: Install K3s Binary
steps.description:
This step installs the K3s binary on your server, which is the core component of K3s.

```bash

curl -sfL https://get.k3s.io | sh -

```

> This command fetches and installs the latest K3s version.

steps.title: Verify K3s Installation
steps.description:
Check if K3s is running correctly by verifying the status of the K3s service.

```bash

systemctl status k3s

```

> Ensure the K3s service is active and running.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your kubeconfig file to interact with your K3s cluster using kubectl.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=/etc/rancher/k3s/k3s.yaml

chmod 600 /etc/rancher/k3s/k3s.yaml

```

> This sets the kubeconfig environment variable.
> Ensure the kubeconfig file has the correct permissions.

steps.title: Verify Nodes in Cluster
steps.description:
Check the nodes in your K3s cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command lists all nodes in the cluster and their status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up additional features like metrics server and RBAC for your K3s cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/k3s-io/k3s/master/manifests/metrics-server.yaml

kubectl create clusterrolebinding default-admin --clusterrole=cluster-admin --serviceaccount=kube-system:default

```

> Install the metrics server for resource monitoring.
> Create a cluster role binding for default service account.

uninstall.title: Uninstall K3s
uninstall.description:
This step removes K3s from your server. Be aware that this will delete all cluster data and resources.

```bash

k3s-uninstall.sh

```

> Run this script to uninstall K3s.

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any orphaned resources that may remain after uninstalling K3s.

```bash

kubectl delete namespace kube-system

```

> This will delete the kube-system namespace and its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade K3s
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. This step will upgrade K3s to the latest version.

```bash

curl -sfL https://get.k3s.io | sh -

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command fetches and installs the latest version of K3s.
> Verify that all nodes are in a ready state after the upgrade.

upgrade.title: Backup Cluster Data
upgrade.description:
Backup your current cluster data before performing the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces -o yaml > k3s-backup.yaml

```

> This command exports all resources to a backup file.

upgrade.title: Post-Upgrade Verification
upgrade.description:
After the upgrade, verify that all components are functioning correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods --all-namespaces

```

> Check the status of all pods to ensure they are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'k3s is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The K3s service may not have started correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'systemctl start k3s' to start the K3s service.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** kubectl may not be installed or not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl or add it to your PATH.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x.x.x.x:6443'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** K3s API server may not be reachable.

**Fix:**
Check if K3s is running and verify your kubeconfig settings.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create pod sandbox'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources on the node.

**Fix:**
Ensure your node meets the minimum resource requirements (2 vCPU, 4GB RAM).

**Affected versions:** <= 1.31

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new CNI plugins, improved metrics server integration.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of certain legacy flags.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure to update your CNI configuration.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' when running 'kubectl get nodes'. All nodes should be in the 'Ready' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your system meets the minimum requirements for installing K3s.
